text: Handling deadlines is an important skill in both work and study. Good time management helps you break a big task into smaller, manageable steps and set realistic mini-deadlines. Prioritization means deciding which tasks are most urgent or important, so you focus on what truly matters first. Scope management involves clearly understanding the project’s limits—knowing what is included and what is not—to avoid taking on extra work that could delay you. Finally, communication is key: if you anticipate a problem, inform your team or supervisor early. By combining these strategies, you can reduce stress and meet your deadlines more effectively.
